competitive_position: Coupa is the incumbent being actively displaced (negative buyer sentiment, post-Thoma Bravo decline) and remains the reference point for budget and fit. Zycus is a confirmed finalist of four with a trusted reference and a value-over-price selection bias in its favour. The single largest competitive blind spot is the three other unnamed RFP finalists, whose positioning and pricing are unknown and will be scored against Zycus when responses are due 30 Jun 2026.

champion.source: Avoma calls 9 Apr and 15 May 2026 (Elie Sauma)
champion.summary: Elie Sauma is an active, transparent champion who brought the deal in via the trusted Paul Hastings reference and confirmed Zycus as a finalist. His advocacy is strong but he is procurement, not finance, and the deal is single-threaded to him; converting his advocacy into access to the finance approver and AP team is the next step.

items.quote: I would like to be able to present back a business case in mid July latest... build the business case internally with our CFO.
items.source: Avoma calls 9 Apr and 15 May 2026 (Elie Sauma)

items.area: AP/finance end-user team not yet engaged on a call
items.quote: Our senior VP of accounting... is 100% looking at automating that process.

items.area: Identities, positioning and pricing of the other 3 RFP finalists unknown
items.quote: I expect you guys to be definitely one of the 4 finalists.

items.area: No quantified ROI/total-cost-of-ownership case yet (transition cost is the key variable for finance)
items.quote: We're gonna have to build in whatever transition cost... that's the part that needs to be part of the total TCO is what I need to present back.

recommended_moves: Submit a tightly scoped RFP response leading on AP automation, OFAC and AI intake by EOD.
Ask Elie to name the finance approver and request a working session to co-build the business case.
Schedule the AP/finance follow-up demo on vendor onboarding, OFAC and AP workflows with the SVP of Accounting invited.
next_step: 6/29 - Zycus submitted our Vendor questions for the RFP - Our response to their RFP is due 6/30 EOD6/9 - RFP Released. Responses due 6/246/1 - No material update. RFP coming Mid June. We are shortlisted5/26 - No material update. RFP coming Mid June. We are shortlisted5/15 - Spoke with Elie (VP of Proc) - Confirmed updated timeline. Long as pricing is comparable to current spend with Coupa, thats fine. He is looking for best fit for their needs and not necessarily a cheaper solution. Gave feedback that previous demo was good but we showed a bit too much. They will provide guidelines for the next demo.Confirmed we will make the top 4 finalists. Releasing RFP by Mid June Latest. Wants to present business case by Mid July. Decision end of July.5/13 - Successful 1st demo.. Lots of positive feedback from the Sitecore team. Coordinating time for follow up demo with New Stakeholders. Tentatively week of 5/18. They will move to a decision in qtr. Strong buying signals. we will invite them to Horizon in next meet5/4 - Completed 1st demo - Moving to follow up demo to go over Isupplier, Irisk and CLM4/28 - First demo scheduled for 4/304/14 - Very detailed intro call done with Steve and team. Next steps is to schedule discovery and an initial demo. This is a Coupa replace that come to us via reference from paul hastings. He said he trusts them very much and the CPO was his mentor/. They want to buy this quarter and move forward to be ready to dump coupa upon renewal. AI is big for them. He said we are automatically a finalist. We have Devika doing the demos as she did Paul Hastings.

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The final budget approver has not been named, introduced, or engaged. Elie Sauma controls the procurement evaluation and is the decision driver, but on 15 May 2026 he made clear the commercial decision goes higher: 'I would like to be able to present back a business case in mid July latest... and get a decision by July', and earlier (9 April 2026) he referenced building the business case 'internally with our CFO because that... can take some time.' He also noted private-equity ownership tightened budgets ('they stepped in here a year ago. So things are super tight there'), signalling cost discipline at the approval level. So the evidence shows a finance approver exists above Elie and will receive the business case in mid-July, but that person is unmapped, unquoted, and unengaged. Identifying and getting a Zycus narrative in front of the economic buyer before the mid-July business case is the highest-value gap to close.
